aggregation_method: End-of-release counts (M221's final JUnit/grep reconciliation), NOT a naive per-milestone sum. Test counts are cumulative suite sizes, so the release value is the final milestone's count; deltas are stated vs v2.2 on the like-for-like method.

tests.go_counting_method: grep -c '^func Test' across all 6 go.mod modules (playthroughs, clerkenstein, alignment, stack-secrets, stack-snapshot, stack-seeding) — the SAME method M217-M221 used.
tests.go_delta_vs_v22: +82 (v2.2 = 1749 by this same like-for-like method; the 1772/1764 recorded at the v2.2 close used a DIFFERENT method and is NOT comparable — see M217 metrics note).
tests.python_counting_method: JUnit XML (pytest --junit-xml), summed testsuite tests/failures/errors/skipped — NEVER grepped from piped stdout (a subtest rolls stdout '650 passed + 9 subtests' to 659/663 collected in XML; the XML is authoritative). M220 + M221 both use this; standardized here for the release.
tests.python_delta_vs_v22: v2.2 = 668 across 3 sections; v2.3 = 1341 across 5 sections (net-new dev-stack + stack-core reach the whole tree for the first time at M220). Like-for-like on the 3 SHARED sections: 668 -> 1105.
tests.python_first_whole_suite_completion: M220 — the whole rext Python tree ran end-to-end for the first time (the 5-milestone dev-stack 'spins forever' chronic was LAND-NOW'd at M220: one missing env var, DEV_SETDRESS_USE_STUB_BINS=1). Re-verified GREEN at M221 close (118 passed / 4 skipped, no hang).
tests.python_count_method_drift_note: Two apparent per-milestone DECREASES are method changes, not shrinking suites: stack-injection 194 (M217 incl-skipped) -> 186 (M218 passing-only); stack-core 152 (M220 passing) -> 182 (M221 JUnit collected incl 30 subtests). Underlying suites only grew. The release standardizes on JUnit COLLECTED counts.
tests.ts_e2e_counting_method: npx playwright test --list (the canonical like-for-like method v2.2 used to record 124). Re-run at HEAD in the release review = 69 + 82 = 151, i.e. +27 vs v2.2 (INCREASE).
tests.ts_e2e_false_regression_note: M219's metrics.json recorded ts = {stack-verify_e2e 38 + playthroughs_e2e 56 = 94} via a DIFFERENT unit/typecheck measure. Copying M219's 94 against v2.2's 124 would register a false 124->94 DECREASE (a blocker under the regression rules). The release records the like-for-like 151 instead; the 94 is a different measure, so NO false regression is filed. (Itself a live D17 instance — a count artifact read as evidence.)

flake_gate: 0 every milestone. close-release triple-clean: 3/3 consecutive full-suite runs (Go 6 modules + Python 5 sections) in randomized order, each in an isolated git worktree. CLEAN 3/3.

supply_chain.govulncheck: No vulnerabilities found on all 6 Go modules.
supply_chain.only_dep_change: golang.org/x/crypto v0.51.0 -> v0.52.0 (INDIRECT, behaviour-neutral, patched) — landed at the M218 close. Zero package.json / requirements changes. Zero new direct Go deps.
supply_chain.licenses: all permissive (MIT/BSD/Apache, no copyleft). The corpus-flagged deliberate dep anthropos-work/ai@v1.40.1 (a v1.10/M45 addition) is present, unchanged, org-internal.

rosetta_release_diff: 108 files, 100% docs/planning (verified in the release review).

regression_rules_applied.test_count_decrease: NONE on the like-for-like methods — Go 1749->1831 (+82); Python 668->1105 on the 3 shared sections; TS e2e 124->151 (+27) via playwright test --list. PASS.
regression_rules_applied.net_new_deps: 0 net-new direct deps (one indirect x/crypto patch bump). PASS.

close_release.review: release-review.md (verdict YELLOW, 0 blockers; the 2 must-fix doc-staleness defects + the deferral/metrics paperwork discharged at close).
close_release.gate_headline: click->ACCESS < 5 s (set M218, re-proven live 8/8 on billion over the tailnet with NO flags: maya-thriving 2.11 s / dan-manager 1.31 s vs baseline 39.45 s / 38.30 s).
